description:
Add a reject variant to the native propose response alongside ok/conflict. It carries a human reason string and NO Task state. The negotiation primitive maps a Reject from a participant whose failure policy is required to an aborted lifecycle event (Task rolled back to pre-event; message carries plugin name + reason); best-effort => warn-and-continue, recorded in sync_status; gating => stage for review. Reject is distinct from Other (wire failure) and Conflict (recoverable field clash).

Honors S2 soft-policy: a required plugin can veto, but an explicit per-invocation operator override (--skip=NAME / the --no-sync analog) still ships, logged in the state-branch commit message per the S11 override log.

WHY: today a plugin can only abort a transition by faking a crash (Other) or a fake conflict. "Block this close because CI is red" deserves a real outcome with a reason, not a degenerate hack. This is what makes arbitrary POLICY — not just side-effects — expressible on a transition.
title: First-class Reject { reason } propose outcome, wired to failure policy
